{{Quote|It looks like your high priest has split from the church.|[[Sonniod]], to Han Solo|<ref name="hsr" />}}
'''Lisstik''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Kamarian Badlander]] who served as a leader for his people in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and his [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] partner [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] visited the [[Kamar/Legends|Kamar]] [[Kamar Badlands|Badlands]] while repairing their [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'', and Lisstik traded with the pair of rare outsiders. Solo and Chewbacca started a business repeatedly showing the [[holodocumentary]] ''[[Varn, World of Water]]'' to the primitive Badlanders, most of whom had never seen anything of the sort in their lives. Lisstik, who was rare among his people in that he spoke some [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], was one of the few to come the first night, and continued to appear every night thereafter as Solo's holos quickly became a sensation among the Badlanders.<ref name="hsr" />

To Solo and Chewbacca's shock, a [[Cult of Varn|cult]] surrounding the holo began to spring up among the Badlanders who were awed by its depiction of a [[Water/Legends|watery]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] wildly different from the desolate Badlands. Lisstik took a place as sort of high [[Priest/Legends|priest]], wearing a burned-out part from the ''Falcon'' as a sign of his office. When the two smugglers changed the holo to the musical drama ''[[Love is Waiting]]'' one night, a riot sprung up among their ersatz congregation, who attacked Lisstik and the visitors in their fury and forced Solo and Chewbacca to flee.<ref name="hsr" />

==Behind the scenes==
Lisstik first appeared in ''[[Han Solo's Revenge]]'', a novel written by [[Brian Daley]] and released in [[1979]].<ref name="hsr" />
